retrieved_docs.title: Enucleation -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: An enucleation is contraindicated in cases of intraocular malignancy with evidence of orbital spread: these patients generally require an exenteration. [15] [16] [17] Patients with sympathetic ophthalmia can be a relative contraindication because the sympathizing eye can ultimately be the eye with better vision, and longitudinal studies have not found a significant advantage of enucleation over evisceration for the prevention of sympathetic ophthalmia. [18] [19] [20] Enucleation can be relatively contraindicated in patients who will experience increased psychological trauma due to the loss of the eye.

retrieved_docs.title: Enucleation -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Enucleation describes the removal of the entire globe, with separation of all connections from the orbit, including optic nerve transection. It is one of the oldest procedures in ophthalmology, with descriptions dating back to 2600BC. [1] The decision for enucleation can be one of the most difficult to make and discuss with the patient. The main indications for enucleation are trauma, painful eye, a blind eye, which is unsightly, intraocular malignancy, and as part of eye donation. Alternatives such as evisceration or exenteration can be considered according to the underlying diagnosis and condition of the eye.

retrieved_docs.title: Sympathetic Ophthalmia -- Treatment / Management -- Surgical
retrieved_docs.content: In cases where the ruptured globe is beyond repair, removal of the eye via evisceration or enucleation is the only option. Significant debate exists as to which procedure is more appropriate. Critics of evisceration argue that evisceration poses a potential risk for disseminating unsuspected uveal malignancy and a theoretical risk of causing sympathetic ophthalmia due to remnant uveal tissues. [95] However, a more recent large retrospective study of patients showed no cases of sympathetic ophthalmia after evisceration. [62] Evisceration is favored since this is technically easier, and preserving the sclera can provide a barrier to prevent the orbital spread of infection. Evisceration may also provide a cosmetic and functional advantage for the patient.

retrieved_docs.title: Enucleation -- Indications -- Trauma
retrieved_docs.content: Primary enucleation Secondary enucleation for sympathetic ophthalmia Autoenucleation (Oedipism)

retrieved_docs.title: Diaphragm Trauma(Archived) -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Penetrating injuries are more commonly reported in the literature and usually present with minimal to no clinical signs, strengthening the argument to assess these wounds with laparoscopy as a standard approach. According to the Western Trauma Association, the controversy as to whether high-resolution MDCT is sufficient to detect diaphragm injuries has not been resolved. They support the findings in the literature, which suggest a 40% incidence of missed injury in the left thoracoabdominal region with penetrating thoracoabdominal wounds. They recommend diagnostic laparoscopy in these patients as the standard modality to further assess for TDI. This is further supported as a practice management guideline by the Eastern Association for the Surgery of Trauma. [2] [5] [2] The concepts of basic laparoscopic surgery should be maintained. Patients are not candidates if they have a clinically significant traumatic brain injury, become unstable, or are suspected of having associated injuries spanning more than one body cavity. [18] A supra-umbilical incision is made, and access is gained into the peritoneum via a Veress needle or cut-down technique. Pneumoperitoneum is achieved, and 2-3 additional 5 mm ports are placed under direct vision adjacent and inferior to the site of injury, most commonly in the mid-clavicular and anterior axillary lines. [18] If technically feasible, the thoracic cavity should be assessed through the diaphragmatic defect without enlarging the defect. This helps to ensure that no thoracic injuries exist and that an iatrogenic pneumothorax has not been created with insufflation. The remainder of the procedure is completed using the classic principles of hernia repair. Penetrating wounds on the right side of the diaphragm are more difficult to evaluate laparoscopically due to the presence of the liver. The Eastern Association for the Surgery of Trauma [2] recommends proceeding with thoracoscopy in these cases. Video-assisted thoracoscopic surgery (VATS) is a reliable option when laparoscopy is suspected to be inadequate, such as in the case of multiple prior abdominal surgeries, suspected right-sided injuries, or less ideal body habitus lending to decreased visibility. [5] [12] The limitation of VATS lies in its inability to simultaneous assess for associated intra-abdominal injuries and how facile the surgeon may be in a trans-thoracic approach.

retrieved_docs.content: A protrusion is when the height of the hernia is less than the length of the base. Extrusion is when the length of the base is less than the height of the hernia. Sequestration is when there is no continuity between the herniated material and the intervertebral disc. [2]

retrieved_docs.content: If observed, foreign bodies should be removed, as they can increase the patient's risk of infection or poor wound healing. Imaging should be obtained if there is any concern about retained foreign bodies.

retrieved_docs.content: Establish hemostasis: Hemostasis should be established to allow for visualization of underlying structures and foreign objects, utilizing direct pressure.

retrieved_docs.content: As noted previously, the opening of the incision is not a recommended process, as this can lead to evisceration and the need for an emergent operative procedure. Instead, radiologic evaluation can guide decision-making as to whether elective/urgent operative intervention is necessary.

retrieved_docs.title: Endoscopic Vein Harvesting -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: From a technical point of view, there are no absolute contraindications for EVH. However, there are some reservations about harvesting the saphenous vein in general, especially in patients with extensive varicosities, deep venous thrombosis, and those where the vessel is of poor quality and would not function as an adequate conduit. [7]

retrieved_docs.content: To best assess the future care requirements of any laceration, the healthcare team must first review the patient's history and thoroughly examine the wound and nearby structures. Pertinent history items include:

retrieved_docs.title: Fascial Dehiscence -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Indications for operative intervention include evisceration, evidence of bowel strangulation/ischemia, bowel obstruction, or undrained infectious collection. Return of herniated bowel contents to the abdominal cavity should occur. Superficial, deep, or organ space infections should be drained and washed out. Nonviable tissues require debridement; bowel resection may be necessary if bowel segments are irreversibly ischemic.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2071
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy-Related Procedures comprise a range of interventions used to manage complex obstetric conditions and facilitate safe delivery. Among these, the surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancy and abdominal pregnancy represents critical operative approaches for addressing abnormal implantation of the embryo, thereby preventing life‑threatening complications for the mother. In addition, Vaginal Assisted Delivery is included as a procedural option that supports the progression of labor and assists in the birth of the infant when additional support is required. Together, these child procedures under the umbrella of Pregnancy-Related Procedures illustrate the spectrum of surgical and assisted techniques employed in modern obstetric care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2791
retrieved_docs.content: Episiotomy and تمزق الأغشية الاصطناعي (artificial membrane rupture) are both classified under الإجراءات العلاجية الخاصة بالتوليد (obstetric therapeutic procedures). By recognizing these interventions as subcomponents of the broader category of obstetric therapeutic care, clinicians can better organize treatment options, understand their related indications, and apply appropriate management strategies within the field of obstetrics.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 206
retrieved_docs.content: “Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery” also includes the concrete example فشل التوليد بالملقط (failed delivery with forceps). Collectively, this hierarchical and functional network links broad procedural categories (ante‑partum, intra‑partum, surgical manipulation, cervical incision, induction, pregnancy‑related care) to their specific clinical actions (amniocentesis, chorionic villus sampling, fetal reduction, various breech and forceps deliveries, obstetrical version techniques, membrane rupture, cervical dilation, trachelotomy, evacuation of retained products, ectopic‑pregnancy surgery, episiotomy repair). Understanding these interrelationships supports clinicians in selecting appropriate obstetric interventions, clarifying synonymous terminology across languages, and optimizing patient care throughout pregnancy management and delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 2 -- Community 1021
retrieved_docs.content: organizes general obstetric procedure categories (إجراء توليدي, Antepartum Procedures, Obstetrics incision) and specific clinical actions—ranging from diagnostic sampling and membrane management to fetal stimulation, reduction of multiple gestations, and surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancies—providing a comprehensive framework for coordinating obstetric care across the ante‑partum, intrapartum, and postpartum periods.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 3 -- Community 1799
retrieved_docs.content: Elective termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري) and forceps delivery (الولادة بالملقط) are clinically associated obstetric interventions. Both procedures are employed within the spectrum of pregnancy management: elective termination represents a planned ending of gestation, while forceps delivery is a method of assisted vaginal birth used when specific maternal or fetal conditions warrant intervention. Their clinical association highlights the range of obstetric care options available to address diverse maternal‑fetal indications, and recognizing this relationship assists clinicians in selecting the most appropriate intervention to optimize outcomes for both mother and newborn.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 1 -- Community 182
retrieved_docs.content: The community organizes obstetric care into a hierarchical taxonomy spanning ante‑partum, intrapartum and postpartum phases. At the apex, the category “إجراء توليدي” (obstetric procedures) is nested under Female genital and obstetric procedures and Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery, and appears as a child of both Antepartum Procedures and the Postpartum obstetric operation list. This category comprises interventions such as Amnioinfusion, Sweeping of membrane, Amniotomy at delivery (linked to the sub‑category Obstetrics incision and classified under Artificial rupture of membranes), Intrapartum Fetal Stimulation, Manual Fetal Rotation, Cesarean hysterectomy, Surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancy – abdominal pregnancy, redukce mnohoplodového těhotenství (reduction of multiple pregnancy), بزل السائل الأمنيوسي (amniocentesis), بضع الفرج (vaginal delivery), البزل القلبي (cardiac puncture) and تفريغ منتجات الحمل المحتجزة (evacuation of retained products of conception).

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2347
retrieved_docs.content: In obstetric practice, the overarching topic “Indications for care in pregnancy; labor; and delivery” includes the specific condition “Malposition; malpresentation,” which is classified as a subcategory (has parent) within these care indications. This relationship highlights that malposition and malpresentation are considered part of the broader set of indications guiding clinical management during pregnancy, labor, and delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 2 -- Community 1021
retrieved_docs.content: cardiac puncture (البزل القلبي), amniocentesis (بزل السائل الأمنيوسي), and vaginal delivery (بضع الفرج). The Obstetrics incision node is associated with the parent activity “artificial rupture of membranes to assist delivery,” highlighting its role in membrane‑rupture techniques used to facilitate birth. Pregnancy‑Related Procedures constitute a major category that aggregates surgical treatment of ectopic and abdominal pregnancies, Vaginal Assisted Delivery, and بضع الفرج, representing diverse modes of delivery assistance. Postpartum obstetric operation is linked to immediate postpartum care and investigation (Zorg en onderzoek onmiddellijk na bevalling), emphasizing early post‑delivery assessment and management. Collectively, these entities and relationships form a structured network that organizes general obstetric procedure categories (إجراء توليدي, Antepartum Procedures, Obstetrics incision) and specific clinical actions—ranging from diagnostic sampling and membrane management to

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 4 -- Community 2858
retrieved_docs.content: Cesarean section is a surgical obstetric procedure in which the operative field directly involves the plodové struktury (fetal structures), as the fetus is delivered through an incision made in the maternal abdomen and uterus. This direct interaction with fetal structures makes cesarean section a critical intervention when vaginal delivery is contraindicated or poses risks to the mother or fetus, and it requires careful coordination between maternal and fetal considerations to ensure optimal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 1 -- Community 163
retrieved_docs.content: is inversely related to “repair of current obstetric laceration,” reflecting a reciprocal classification within the repair hierarchy. Collectively, these entities illustrate a comprehensive hierarchical framework in which antenatal/postnatal care, delivery findings, surgical manipulation techniques, obstetric manipulations, and repair procedures are interrelated, guiding clinical decision‑making and procedural selection across diverse birth scenarios.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 1 -- Community 94
retrieved_docs.content: as a tissue‑excising procedure, and “Endometrial sampling (biopsy) with or without endocervical sampling, without cervical dilation, any method” is documented as “خزعة بطانة الرحم” and performed by obstetrics‑and‑gynecology physicians. Therapeutic and surgical interventions are grouped under “Other or therapeutic procedures; female organs”. Construction of an artificial vagina is categorized as a form of surgical construction within this group. Laparoscopic treatment of ectopic pregnancy without salpingectomy and/or oophorectomy is a specific method under the broader class “Surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancy; abdominal pregnancy”. Salpingo‑oophorectomy (complete or partial, unilateral or bilateral) and “Excision of cervical stump by abdominal approach” are also linked to the Obstetrics & Gynecology specialty. Obstetric repairs are organized as “OBSTETRICS: SURGICAL REPAIRS, CLOSURES AND RECONSTRUCTIONS”, a subcategory of “repair of current obstetric laceration”. Exclusion rules

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 1 -- Community 132
retrieved_docs.content: The community defines **إجراء توليدي** as a generic obstetric procedure that encompasses a broad spectrum of ante‑, intra‑, and postpartum interventions. It is classified as a child of the broader categories *Female genital and obstetric procedures* and *Other therapeutic obstetrical procedures* and serves as a parent to numerous specific techniques, including **amniinfusion**, **amniotomy at delivery**, **amniocentesis** (بزل السائل الأمنيوسي), **cardiac puncture** (البزل القلبي), **vaginal delivery** (بضع الفرج), **evacuation of retained products of conception** (تفريغ منتجات الحمل المحتجزة), **fetal monitoring** (مراقبة الجنين), **reduction of multiple pregnancy** (redukce mnohoplodového těhotenství), **cervical cerclage** (إجراء تطويق عنق الرحم), and various **postoperative obstetric operations**. Within **Antepartum Procedures**, the sub‑category “Application, Insertion, Removal” includes **fetal reduction**, **amniocentesis**, **placental villi biopsy** (خزعة الزغابات المشيمية)

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 1640
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy‑Related Procedures encompass a range of interventions that are linked to the outcome of having a child. This category includes the surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancy and abdominal pregnancy, as well as Vaginal Assisted Delivery, both of which are performed to manage complex pregnancy situations and facilitate successful childbirth. In addition, the subcategory بضع الفرج falls under Pregnancy‑Related Procedures, further illustrating the breadth of techniques aimed at ensuring a viable delivery. All of these procedures share the common purpose of supporting the continuation of pregnancy to the point of delivering a child.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1850
retrieved_docs.content: Abdominal pregnancy (حمل منتبذ) is clinically associated with laparotomy (شق البطن), because surgical entry into the abdominal cavity is frequently required for the diagnosis, evaluation, and definitive management of this rare ectopic gestation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 3 -- Community 2022
retrieved_docs.content: Exploratory laparotomy and ثقب معوي (intestinal perforation) are frequently observed together in clinical practice. An exploratory laparotomy is often performed when a ثقب معوي is suspected or identified, as the surgical approach allows direct visualization and management of the perforated bowel. Recognizing the co‑occurrence of these conditions is important for timely diagnosis and appropriate operative intervention, which can reduce morbidity and improve patient outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 27 -- Level 1 -- Community 206
retrieved_docs.content: and monitoring procedure placed within the broader group of imaging procedures related to genital and breast organs (إجراءات تصوير متعلقة بالأعضاء التناسلية والثدي). Cardiac puncture (البزل القلبي), endometrial sampling (with or without endocervical sampling), and laparoscopic treatment of ectopic or abdominal pregnancy (performed without salpingectomy or oophorectomy) are included. Laparoscopic treatment of ectopic pregnancy and surgical treatment of abdominal pregnancy are child concepts of Pregnancy‑Related Procedures, which also contains vaginal assisted delivery, illustrating the spectrum from ectopic management to assisted vaginal birth. Repair of current obstetric lacerations is a broader category of obstetric surgical repairs, closures and reconstructions; it is linked inversely to “Repair of obstetric laceration” and is considered a uterine procedure. The Czech‑language category porodnické chirurgické výkony (obstetric surgical procedures) nests these surgical repairs,
